step_by_step_thinking:
(Do NOT include any markdown.)

I agree with the original conclusion that a case-control study is appropriate because it enables rapid assessment through retrospective data collection from patients already diagnosed with lung cancer. Unlike prospective cohort studies which take many years to accumulate sufficient events, case-control studies can identify past exposures like smoking among those who have developed cancer much faster due to their backward-looking nature. <B>
